fault_hypothesis: The expiry condition on line 70 uses strict greater-than (`now > adjusted_deadline`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= adjusted_deadline`), so an entry accessed exactly at its deadline is incorrectly treated as still valid.

constraints.intent: When now equals adjusted_deadline the condition must evaluate to expired (True), but the strict > misses this boundary case.

spec.expr: not (now == adjusted_deadline__1) or (now > adjusted_deadline__1 or now == adjusted_deadline__1)

constraints.intent: After computing adjusted_deadline, if now equals it the entry should be considered expired, so the expiry check must use >= not >.
Sanity check that the boundary case now == adjusted_deadline__1 is covered by the expiry branch (trivially true structurally, but forces evaluation at the boundary).
In the else branch (entry not expired), now must be strictly less than adjusted_deadline; if now == adjusted_deadline we should not reach this branch.
